info.title: DA API V1 Reference
info.description:
# Developer Notes ## Introduction Digitales Autohaus (DA) is a multi tenant web application where each tenant can map his organizational structure to instances of the application. A tenant (labeled as _group_ in DA terms) can have multiple sites respectively facilities (labeled _center_). In order to address an instance of the application one has to provide both parameters. To access an instance in a webbrowser the URL has to be composed in the following way: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic` In all calls to the DA REST-API the target instance has to be identified by passing the header parameters `group` and `center`. The main endpoint for the DA REST-API is `https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1`. ## API lifecycle We have established a basic lifecycle management for our APIs. Currently we differentiate a `beta` and a `stable` branch of each major API version. The stable API will be accessible by the main endpoints (like `/da/v1` or `/da/v2`). The beta versions of the API will be accessible by special beta endpoints (like `/da/v1-beta` or `/da/v2-beta`). Routes under the beta branch could be changing and may be not fully functional. Beta routes are meant to be an early access view for our API partners and should be used to pass feedback back to us. Once routes are mature they will be migrated to the stable branch and are from then on supported for a longer period of time. We will do our very best to always be backwards compatible with all our changes to the API, but if there are breaking changes necessary, we will inform our partners with an adequately in advance. When beta routes have been marked as stable, we will support those routes as well under the beta branch and the stable branch for a limited time in which the use of the beta branch is deprecated but possible. After that the beta routes are taken offline and only the stable ones will be accessible. Details on the different support periods will be published here shortly. ## Authentification and authorization Authorization of an external application which uses the DA REST-API will be carried out over standard OAuth2 protocol. The authorization flow used in this case is the OAuth2 _password grant_ method. For this OAuth flow the following details are required: * Client ID * Client name * Client secret * Username and password of a registered user in the target instance
(**Note 1:** The user has to be unique in the context of the target instance!) (**Note 2:** You have to pass the email address of the user to the API!) The `grant_type` will always be `password` and the necessary scopes will be implicitly provided by the authorization endpoint. The details for the OAuth client, e.g. client ID, name and secret will be provided by Gudat Solutions to each registered developer. These will always be the same for all instances and have to be protected by the external developer. By receiving the client credentials an external developer is granted access to the REST-API but an individual authorization in each instance by the end customers administrator is still mandatory. Additionally credentials of registered user in the target instance are required which will relate the user identity to each call of the REST API. It depends on the use case and the implementation of the external application which user would be appropriate. One can use a _system account_ which will be used by default for all calls to the API or individual user accounts. It is the responsibility of an external developer to resolve that in his implementation. With this information the application has to call the authorization endpoint of the API and receive an authorization token. This token has to be passed in each subsequent call to the API as a _bearer token_ in the authorization header of the request. Each generated token holds an expiration time after which it cannot be used any more. After expiration a new token has to generated either by following the outlined process or by using a _refresh token_ which will be provided by the call to the authorization endpoint as well. ## Token expiration After generating a token, the access token expires in 1 year, the refresh token expiration is increased by 30 days. When the token is expired the server will send the status code **401** - Unauthorized ##### Recommended authentification implementation After getting a 401 from the server, try to refresh the token. By getting a 401 at the refresh process (this is possible if the refresh token has also expired or the set of scopes has changed) you have to generate a new access token. ### Further information * [OAuth 2.0 Password Grant](https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/password/) * [OAuth 2.0 Refresh Token](https://oauth.net/2/grant-types/refresh-token/) ## Current state of the API The DA API is still evolving but the published parts for _customers_ and _vehicles_ are meant to be stable in their currently documented form. Especially details regarding the authentication and authorization flow however could be changing for a certain time. Likewise we expect changes to the developer portal itself. Gudat Solutions will notify each registered developer on a short-term basis in case of a significant change. ## Demo instance Gudat Solutions provides a demo instance for implementation and integration purposes. This demo instance can be accessed by using the following parameters: * group = demo * center = api Currently the instance is not accessible by a webbrowser but only by the API REST endpoint. For testing purposes the demo instances will be seeded with demo master data (customers and vehicles) and will be reset periodically. If you need access to the application instance from a webbrowser or an inventory of the generated demo data, please get in contact with us. ## Getting help In order to get help from our developer support team, you can send an email to developer@digitalesautohaus.de. Please provide us with the following details: * Description of the expected result of the request * Description of the response of the request * Ideally an example request ([cURL](https://curl.se/) oder [Postman](https://www.postman.com/)) * URL of the target instance (including domain und endpoint) * HTTP method * Body payload * HTTP header (including bearer token) * Plain response * HTTP status code # Deeplink integration Beside the API there are some ways of integrating DA by deeplinks. For this kind of integration you have to know the URL of the DA target instance you want interface with. Example: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/` Where `group` and `center` depend on the customers instance and `request` can be replaced with one of the following options. The viewId is the ID of the view you want to open. It could be the ID of calendar, table or dashboard view. You can get the ID of the view by inspecting the URL of the view in the browser. ## Deeplinks dossier and appointments ### Creating a blank new dossier `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?caseId=new` This will open the new dossier drawer for the current date. Optionally you can provide a date only as last query parameter. The date has to be in the format `YYYY-MM-DD` and will be used as current date in the view: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?caseId=new&date=2024-03-10` ### Creating a new dossier with given data You can pass JSON payload as a query parameter to prefill the new dossier drawer: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?caseData={}` For details regarding the payload, have a look at the `service_events` API documentation. ### Creating a new dossier with given customer/ vehicle You can create a new dossier drawer and directly pass parameters for master data search: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?searchCustomerNumber=` `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?searchLicensePlate=` `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?searchVin=` ### Open an existing dossier If you know the ID of the dossier, you can directly open the drawer filled with case details: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/views/?caseId=` ## Deeplinks local wheels view ### Directly opening a predefined filter view in the local wheels view Identically to the dashboard, each predefined filter can be directly accessed by passing its name as query parameter: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/da/index.html#/local-wheel/search?filter=` ## Deeplinks order ### Open a dossier drawer with existing external order or import it from the Application You can open a dossier drawer with an existing order by passing the external order reference and the application ID as query parameters or import it from the external application if it does not exist yet.: `https://werkstattplanung.net//
/kic/#/?externalOrderRef=&application_id=` # Workflows You can configure Workflows and listen to DA events for example to send an Email after creating an appointment.\ DA > Management > Workflows ## DA Webhook You can listen to most of the events triggered in the DA and get the related Data of the event fired to a pre configured Endpoint.\ For this function you have to configure a Workflow and select the action "DA - Webhook" (DA > Management > Workflows). The configured endpoint (URL) will be called via HTTP POST request.\ The request body contains the relevant data in JSON format.\ You will get the full data every time (not only the changed attributes). Body: ``` event: The event trigger -> created, updated, deleted, saved object: - type: The type of the triggered object - attributes: Attributes of the triggered object user - id: ID of trigger user - name: Name of trigger user data: This additional data is configurable in the DA Webhook action - dossier - contact - vehicle - appointments - orders - workshop_tasks - replacementVehicle ``` Example: ``` { "event": "updated", "object": { "type": "Statable", "attributes": { "id": 4, "state_id": 3, "statable_id": 4, "statable_type": "App\\Models\\Dossier", "changed_by": 1, "created_by": 3, "created_at": "2023-02-23 10:20:31", "updated_at": "2023-02-23 11:48:25", } }, "user": { "id": 1, "name": "Gudat" }, "data": { "dossier": { "id": 4, "note": "Cockpitreinigung", "completion_date_time": "2023-02-23 16:00:00", "preorder_verified": false, "maintenance_booklet": 0, "vehicle_registration_orignal": 0, "requested_call_back": 0, "requested_care_program": 0, "mobility_addressed": false, "et360_pick_up": null, "et360_drop_off": null, "et360_auf_achse": false, "handled_by": null, "changed_by": 1, "vehicle_id": 4, "contact_id": 4, "parking_space": null, "service_consultation_id": null, Dolorem sint quos quis beatae.", "dse_email": false, "created_by": 3, "changed_by": 3, "application_id": "da" }, "vehicle": { "id": 4, "external_ref": "U0XAPMPM4PPY86MPC", "vehicle_identification_number": "U0XAPMPM4PPY86MPC", "first_registration_date": "2003-02-23", "license_plate": "HH-B 1197", "brand": "Opel", "model": "EL", "model_type": "Panda", "manufacturer_key_number": "0590", "type_key_number": "328", "mileage": "19614", "general_inspection_date": "2023-02-23", "created_by": 3, "changed_by": 3, "application_id": "da" }, "appointments": [ { "id": 7, "start_date_time": "2023-03-05 08:00:00", "end_date_time": "2023-03-05 08:30:00", "dossier_id": 4, "resource_id": 2, "event_type_id": null, "note": null, "external_booking_number": null, "start_date_time": "2023-02-23 11:45:00", "end_date_time": "2023-02-23 12:00:00", "dossier_id": 4, "resource_id": 2, "event_type_id": 2, "note": null, "external_booking_number": null, "external_booking_id": null, "type": "service", "address_id": null, "created_by": 3, "changed_by": 1, "created_at": "2023-02-23 10:20:31", "updated_at": "2023-02-23 11:47:20" } ], "orders": [ { "id": 4, "number": "8865", "external_ref": "8865", "dossier_id": 4, "insurer_id": null, "insurer_reference": null, "auto_repair_manager_id": null, "auto_repair_manager_reference": null, "application_id": "da", "created_by": 3, "created_at": "2023-02-23 10:20:31", "updated_at": "2023-02-23 10:20:31", "order_type_id": null, "is_controlled": false, "is_huk_relevant": false } ], "workshop_tasks": [ { "id": 5, "dossier_id": 4, "workshop_team_id": 1, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "start_date": "2023-02-23", "start_time": "11:45:00", "work_load": 8, "work_state": 3, "workshop_service_id": 4, "description": null, "resource_id": 1, "order_id": null, "workshop_task_package_id": null, "team_order": 4, "resource_order": 0, "vat": null, "price_netto": null } ], "replacement_vehicle": { "id": 1, "replacement_vehicle_vin": "12345678910111213", "dossier_id": 4, "start_date": "2023-02-23 11:45:00", "end_date": "2023-03-05 08:30:00", "driver": null, "last_name": "Hohmann", "first_name": "Arnulf", "company": null, "phone_number": "00331 38118", "mobile_number": "+49(0) 044359944", "email": "jochen.oswald@example.net", "customer_id": "1672", "rental_contract_id": "1677149250", "rental_contract_date": "2023-02-23", "mileage": 0, "comment": null, "salutation_id": null, "transfer_location": null, "return_location": null, "event_type": null, "vehicle_status": null, "vehicle_custom_status": null, "created_by": 1, "driver_license_number": null, "mileage_free": null, "daily_rate": null, "mileage_rate": null, "contribution": null, "insurance": null, "tank_capacity": null, "tarif": null, "tarif_typ": null, "mileage_out": 0, "mileage_return": 0, "city": "Garmisch-Partenkirchen", "zip_code": "74942", "street": "Maja-Beer-Weg 4b", "modified": false, "vehicle_type": null, "miete6_is_synced": null, "created_at": "2023-02-23 11:47:34", "updated_at": "2023-02-23 11:47:34" } } } } ``` # Online appointment booking (OAB) portal iframe integration For integrating the DA OAB portal by iframe you have to respect the following issues. If you want to use the embedded version of our booking portal, get in touch with us. ## Design customization It is possible to create a CSS for styling the embedded version of the OAB. Only one CSS file will be imported. For the creation of your design, the booking URL has to be called passing the query parameter `embedded_mode=true`. ## Query parameters Append the following query parameters to the booking URL: `embedded_mode=true` Only a stripped down version of the booking page will be displayed, without borders and images. `parent_url={parent URL of the embedding page}` postMessages will be send to this URL. See also: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage ## PostMessages Following events will be send to the `parent_url` via postMessage: * On a JS resize event: `document.body.clientHeight + 40` * On change of the URL/ route: `routeChanged` * After successfull booking of the appointment: `EventDateTime:--
::` ## Online Booking API with Booking Configuration For a convient use of the online booking API you can pre configure a Booking Configuration with following options: * EventType (relevant for appointment duration) * Bookable mobilities * Bookable resources * Bookable workshop services * Business hours * Ignoring work unit by calculating free slots * Grid length * Offset To use a pre defined configuration you have to pass the Query Parameter `booking_configuration_id=` to every Request of the online booking. \ (The ID will show up in the configuration view) \ By passing this Parameter the API will include the configured options in all their operations. # API Examples ## Getting an access token ``` curl --location --request POST 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/oauth/token' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --data-urlencode 'grant_type=password' \ --data-urlencode 'client_id=' \ --data-urlencode 'client_secret=' \ --data-urlencode 'username=' \ --data-urlencode 'password=' ``` Sample Response ```json { "token_type":"Bearer", "expires_in":31536000, "access_token":, "refresh_token":, "scope":"*" } ``` For the following requests you will need the access_token! We recommend if your access token is expired, fetch a new one instead of refreshing. ## Getting case and appointment information In the following request we are going to fetch all cases (or dossiers in our language) in a given date range. For fetching the dossiers of a single day, please provide the day your are looking for as `start` and the day after this one as `end`. By specifying `include=dossier.statables` you will get all related states of the dossier merged into the response body. ``` curl --location -g --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/service_events?filter[inRange]=2021-09-21,2021-09-23&include=dossier.statables' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' ``` Sample Response ```json { "current_page": 1, "data": [ { "id": 220, "note": null, "completion_date_time": null, "preorder_verified": false, "maintenance_booklet": 0, "vehicle_registration_orignal": 0, "requested_call_back": 0, "requested_care_program": 0, "mobility_addressed": false, "mobility_id": null, "mobility_driving_permit": false, "mobility_excess": false, "mobility_others": null, "note_workshop": null, "note_question": null, "created_by": 1, "created_at": "2021-09-30 08:08:23", "driver": null, "driver_phone_number": null, "in_open_list": false, "divergent_billing_address": null, "updated_at": "2021-09-30 08:33:06", "imported_at": null, "reparation_start_date_time": null, "application_id": "da", "external_ref": "4770", "reminder": null, "deposit_key_number": null, "canceled_at": null, "evaluated_by_customer_delta": false, "et360_pick_up": null, "et360_drop_off": null, "handled_by": 1, "et360_auf_achse": false, "changed_by": 1, "parking_space": null, "service_consultation_id": null, "care_program_id": null, "is_attendant": false, "is_skipping_reception": false, "is_vehicle_registration_present": false, "vehicle_id": 209, "contact_id": 212, "statables": [ { "id": 208, "state_id": 17, "statable_id": 220, "statable_type": "App\\Models\\Dossier", "changed_by": 1, "created_by": 1, "created_at": "2021-09-30 08:28:49", "updated_at": "2021-09-30 08:28:49" }, { "id": 209, "state_id": 24, "statable_id": 220, "statable_type": "App\\Models\\Dossier", "changed_by": 1, "created_by": 1, "created_at": "2021-09-30 08:28:49", "updated_at": "2021-09-30 08:28:49" } ], "start_date_time": "2021-09-30 11:00:00", "end_date_time": "2021-09-30 11:15:00", "resource_id": 1, "event_type_id": null, "external_booking_number": null, "pick_up_date_time": null, "pick_up_address": null, "bring_date_time": null, "bring_address": null, "finish_pickup_date_time": "2021-10-12 07:00:00", "workshop_tasks": [ { "id": 424, "dossier_id": 220, "workshop_team_id": 1, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "start_date": "2021-09-30", "start_time": null, "work_load": 8, "work_state": 3, "workshop_service_id": 1, "description": null, "resource_id": null, "order_id": null, "workshop_task_package_id": null, "team_order": 0, "resource_order": 0, "start_date_time": null }, { "id": 425, "dossier_id": 220, "workshop_team_id": 1, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "start_date": "2021-09-30", "start_time": null, "work_load": 12, "work_state": 3, "workshop_service_id": 2, "description": null, "resource_id": null, "order_id": null, "workshop_task_package_id": null, "team_order": 0, "resource_order": 0, "start_date_time": null } ], "vehicle_identification_number": "IK125XMQ2KK211497", "first_registration_date": "2000-11-06", "license_plate": "B-AW 9976", "brand": null, "model": null, "model_type": "Golf 8 GTI", "manufacturer_key_number": null, "type_key_number": null, "mileage": "36287", "general_inspection_date": "1977-02-02", "account_number": "4770", "salutation_id": 6, "last_name": "Meier", "first_name": "Gudrun", "company": null, "phone_number": "+49(0)9430 74557", "mobile_number": "+49 (0) 3773 844710", "email": "nancy22@example.org", "street": "Hasan-Paul-Gasse 8", "zip_code": "63526", "city": "H\u00fcrth", "send_event_reminder": false, "dse_phone": false, "dse_sms": false, "dse_fax": true, "dse_post": false, "dse_opt_in": true, "dse_document": "1", "dse_origin": "1", "dse_submission": "2004-04-15", "dse_expiry": "1981-09-21", "dse_ccs_info": "0", "dse_email": true, "customer_number": "4770", "vehicle_type": "Golf 8 GTI" } ], "first_page_url": "https:\/\/werkstattplanung.net\/demo\/api\/kic\/api\/gateway\/service_events?filter%5BinRange%5D=2021-09-29%2C2021-10-01&include=dossier.statables%2Cstatables&page=1", "from": 1, "next_page_url": null, "path": "https:\/\/werkstattplanung.net\/demo\/api\/kic\/api\/gateway\/service_events", "per_page": 100, "prev_page_url": null, "to": 1 } ``` As you can see this is quite a long response body, because we merge much of the data which exists on our side into the reponse of this single request in order to minimize the number of requests to the API. Note on the states: In the `statables` array you will get all states related to the dossier. If you need further information the individual states or state categories you have to query that by `/states` or `/state_categories` routes (see below). ## Working with states Since the state system in DA is fully customizable, you can't rely on specific states to be existent in each instance. In DA the user can create several `state_categories` which can be connected to objects like a dossier, an order or an appointment. Each state category can contain as many `states` as needed. Therefore you have to query the instance of DA you are working with, which state categories and related states have been created by the user. ### Fetching all availabe states ``` curl --location --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/states' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' ``` Sample Response ```json { "current_page": 1, "data": [ { "id": 1, "name": "Test-Status", "type": "order", "is_immutable": false, "created_at": "2019-12-17 11:53:33", "updated_at": "2019-12-17 11:53:33" }, { "id": 2, "name": "Vorgangsstatus", "type": "dossier", "is_immutable": true, "created_at": "2020-02-25 14:54:19", "updated_at": "2020-02-25 14:54:19" }, { "id": 3, "name": "Marketing", "type": "dossier", "is_immutable": false, "created_at": "2021-06-23 10:07:36", "updated_at": "2021-06-23 10:07:36" }, { "id": 4, "name": "Serviceberatung", "type": "appointment", "is_immutable": false, "created_at": "2021-09-30 08:29:48", "updated_at": "2021-09-30 08:29:48" } ], "first_page_url": "https:\/\/werkstattplanung.net\/demo\/api\/kic\/api\/gateway\/state_categories?page=1", "from": 1, "next_page_url": null, "path": "https:\/\/werkstattplanung.net\/demo\/api\/kic\/api\/gateway\/state_categories", "per_page": 100, "prev_page_url": null, "to": 4 } ``` In that sample response we see that there are four state categories configured in that instance. There are two categories related to dossiers (*Vorgangsstatus* and *Marketig*), one to orders (*Test-Status*) and one to appointments (*Serviceberatung*). ### Fetching all states of a state category To get a list of all states in a state category, you can filter by a given id: ``` curl --location --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/states?filter[state_category_id]=2' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' ``` Sample response ```json { "current_page": 1, "data": [ { "id": 10, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "state_category_id": 2, "color": "#FFFF8D", "name": "Auftrag vorbereitet", "is_done": false, "order": 100, "is_immutable": false, "completion_status_id": 14, "created_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07", "updated_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07" }, { "id": 11, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "state_category_id": 2, "color": "#EA80FC", "name": "Teile vorr\u00e4tig", "is_done": false, "order": 700, "is_immutable": false, "completion_status_id": 57, "created_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07", "updated_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07" }, { "id": 12, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "state_category_id": 2, "color": "#FFFF8D", "name": "Kunde im Haus", "is_done": false, "order": 800, "is_immutable": false, "is_done": false, "order": 400, "is_immutable": false, "completion_status_id": 74, "created_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07", "updated_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07" }, { "id": 20, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "state_category_id": 2, "color": "#8C9EFF", "name": "Kunde informiert", "is_done": false, "order": 500, "is_immutable": false, "completion_status_id": 75, "created_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07", "updated_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07" }, { "id": 21, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "state_category_id": 2, "color": "#8C9EFF", "name": "Fahrzeug abgeholt", "is_done": false, "order": 600, "is_immutable": false, "completion_status_id": 76, "created_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07", "updated_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07" } ], "first_page_url": "https:\/\/werkstattplanung.net\/demo\/api\/kic\/api\/gateway\/states?filter%5Bstate_category_id%5D=2&page=1", "from": 1, "next_page_url": null, "path": "https:\/\/werkstattplanung.net\/demo\/api\/kic\/api\/gateway\/states", "per_page": 100, "prev_page_url": null, "to": 12 } ``` ### Getting details for a state To query the API for details regarding a single state, say state with id 17 of the `/service_appointments` example above: ``` curl --location --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/states/17' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' ``` Sample Response ```json { "id": 17, "external_ref": null, "application_id": null, "state_category_id": 2, "color": "#8C9EFF", "name": "Endkontrolle", "is_done": false, "order": 200, "is_immutable": false, "completion_status_id": 72, "created_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07", "updated_at": "2019-05-14 14:22:07" } ``` To load an array of all available states, simply skip the ressource id and use the `/states` route. ## Using the status trigger logic In order for a simple external state change of a case in DA there is a functionality calles `status_triggers`. This is typically used by handheld barcode scanners or license plate detection systems. For this mechanism to work one has to configure all possible external trigger sources in the DA application under `Verwaltung > Termin > Statustrigger`. ![status_trigger_list.jpg](images/status_trigger_list.jpg) When the external source is triggered by calling the `status_triggers` route, internally a matching case is searched using the passed arguments (see below). If a case is found, the configured state in the status trigger configuration will be set. Currently there are two possible types of trigger sources: * camera * scanner Trigger type `camera` will seach for a case for a vehicle with the given license plate which has an delivery <= *last month* and a completion >= *last month*. \ Trigger type `scanner` will search for a case for a vehicle with the given vin and has a delivery >= *last month* and a completion >= *last month*. ![status_trigger_config_camera.jpg](images/status_trigger_config_camera.jpg) In the screenshot above there is an external trigger configured for a camera which will set the state to "Vorgangsstatus: Kunde im Haus" if the camera with id 1 will detect a license plate. ### Changing a state by type camera ``` curl --location --request POST 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/status_triggers' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' --data-raw '{ "type": "camera", "status_trigger_configuration_id": {your configured status trigger id}, "data": { "license_plate": "{license plate}" } }' ``` ### Changing a state by type scanner ``` curl --location --request POST 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/status_triggers' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' --data-raw '{ "type": "scanner", "status_trigger_configuration_id": {your configured status trigger id}, "data": { "vin": "{vin}" } }' ``` ### Changing a state without trigger configuration If you don't want to configure a trigger you can pass the state id instead: ``` curl --location --request POST 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/status_triggers' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' --data-raw '{ "type": "camera", "status_id": 4, "data": { "license_plate": "{license plate}" } }' ``` ### Fetching free slots for appointment booking To include the work unit calculation you have to pass the workshop_service_ids ``` curl --location -g --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/free_slots?start_date=2022-06-21&end_date=2022-06-23&workshop_service_ids[0]=3&workshop_service_ids[1]=6&duration=30&grid=30' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --data-raw '' ``` ### Fetching free slots for appointment booking using a Booking Configuration ``` curl --location -g --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/free_slots?start_date=2022-06-21&end_date=2022-06-23&workshop_service_ids[0]=3&workshop_service_ids[1]=6&booking_configuration_id=' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --data-raw ''' ``` ## Creating a booking appointment with attachments, workshop tasks and states (statables) ``` curl --location --request POST 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/service_events' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --data-raw '{ "start_date_time": "2022-06-23 13:00:00", "end_date_time": "2022-06-23 13:30:00", "resource_id": 2, "last_name": "Test", "first_name": "Tom", "mobile_number": "01784563226", "email": "tom.test@gmx.de", "street": "Teststr. 1", "zip_code": "1337", "city": "Testtown", "license_plate": "BO-GU 1234", "vehicle_identification_number": "WVWZZZ1JZ3W386752", "first_registration_date": "2010-02-03", "brand": "VW", "model": "Golf", "model_type": "2.0 GTI", "mileage": 39541, "manufacturer_key_number": "0603", "type_key_number": "348", "attachments": [ { "name": "Kratzer vorne rechts", "base64_encoded_content": "" } ], "workshop_tasks": [ { "workshop_service_id": 3, "start_date_time": "2022-06-23 13:00:00", "description": "Mit Vorabcheck!" }, { "workshop_service_id": 6, "start_date_time": "2022-06-23 13:00:00" } ], "statables": [ { "state_id": 1 }, { "state_id": 2 } ] }' ``` ## Cancel an existing Service Event You can revert the cancelation in the DA itself ``` curl --location --request DELETE 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/service_events/' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --data-raw '' ``` ## Fetching states of an existing Service Event A Service Event can habe multiple states ``` curl --location --request GET 'https://api.werkstattplanung.net/da/v1/service_events//states' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'group: ' \ --header 'center: ' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer ' \ --data-raw '' ``` # API Changelog ## [1.9] - 2024-11-18 - Added new endpoint to query documents based on an order_number ## [1.8] - 2024-09-03 - Added bookable mobilities to Booking Configuration ## [1.7] - 2024-04-16 - Added documentation for creating, updating and deleting Statables - Added documentation for fetching a Booking Configuration by ID - Specified what parameters are valid as `filter[name]` for fetching Statables ## [1.6] - 2024-04-11 - Added `description` Parameter in workshop_tasks - Added `node` Parameter in service_events ## [1.5] - 2022-08-01 - Added `booking_configuration_id` Parameter in online appointment booking context - Added additional examples ## [1.4] - 2020-07-27 - Moved online booking beta routes to stable ## [1.3] - 2020-02-28 **stable** **beta** - Remove completion_status_id by ServiceEvent - Added statables reference to ServiceEvent - Removed Route /completion_statuses ## [1.2] - 2020-01-23 **stable** **beta** - Added /v1-beta routes - /states - /statables - /state_categories ## [1.1] - 2019-08-08 **stable** **beta** - Added GET method for service_events route ## [1.0] - 2019-07-10 **stable** - extended /customers query parameter `type` by `phone_numbers` to search for phone or mobile number **beta** - Added /v1-beta routes - /center_config - /free_slots - /service_events - /vehicle_types - /event_types - /resources - /workshop_services - /salutations - /completion_statuses - /mobilities

post.description:
An incoming StatusTrigger will change the state on an event detected by the data property to the given or configured state you can define a configuration for the StatusTrigger in the DA Application and can use the ID for the property status_trigger_configuration_id

Type camera: The license_plate data property is required, the detected dossier must match the given license plate and must be today

Type scanner: The vin data property is required, the detected dossier must match the given vin, start date time of the service appointment >= "last month" and completion date time >= "today"

post.summary: Upload and attach a document to a dossier
post.description:
Uploads a file of any format and attaches it to a specified dossier. You can specify the target dossier in one of two ways: 
 - By providing a direct dossier_id. 
 - By providing an order_number. In this case, the system will find the most recently created order with that order_number and use its associated dossier. Optionally, you can further filter the order by application_id.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: The group name of the DA instance to be accessed

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center name of the DA instance to be accessed

200.description: Success - Document uploaded and attached to dossier

object_id.description: Dossier ID the document is attached to

file_type.description: MIME type of the uploaded file

404.description: Not Found - Order not found for the provided order_number

error.example: Order not found for the provided order_number

409.description: Conflict - Document with same name already exists

error.example: Document with this name already exists for this dossier

get.summary: Get a list of documents based on order_number

parameters.name: order_number
parameters.description: Order number

salutation.description: As text, we will try to set the correct id by the name property of the Salutation model

is_watermarked.description: Whether the document is watermarked (0 = false, 1 = true)

file_size.description: Size of the file in bytes

view_url.description: URL to view the document locally

api_gateway_view_url.description: API gateway URL to view the document

external_ref.description: External reference for the workshop task

work_state.description: Workshop states, allowed values -> 0 - Break, 1 - In Work, 2 - Done, 3 - New

description.example: This task involves replacing the brake pads and rotors.

deposit_key_number.description: The number of the car key

auto_customer_data.description: If set we will try to autocomplete the customer and vehicle data by the field 'vehicle_identification_number' or 'license_plate'

start_date_time.description: Start date and time for the car checkin [Y-m-d H:i:s]

end_date_time.description:
End date and time for the car checkin.
Actually this is the duration which is reserved in the service consultants calendar for customer care
and the initial inspection of the car.
[Y-m-d H:i:s]'

completion_date_time.description: Planned completion date and time for the order [Y-m-d H:i:s]

pick_up_date_time.description: Date and time for the car pick-up, in case a pick-up and delivery service was booked by the customer [Y-m-d H:i:s]

bring_date_time.description: Date and time for the car delivery to the customer, in case a pick-up and delivery service was booked [Y-m-d H:i:s]

finish_pickup_date_time.description: Date and time when the customer picks up the car [Y-m-d H:i:s]

property.description: The error text of the failed property validation

number.description: The unique Number of the order

auto_repair_manager_id.description: Reference to a auto repair manager

type.description: The input type "camera" or "scanner" - required without status_trigger_configuration_id

status_id.description: Reference to the Status Model - required without status_trigger_configuration_id

status_trigger_configuration_id.description: The "ID" of you defined StatusTrigger in DA Application if set the fields type and status_id will be ignored

data.description: The Payload to identify the event

file.description: The file to upload (any format supported)

dossier_id.description: ID of the dossier to attach the document to

name.description: Custom name for the document (optional, defaults to original filename)

tag.description: Tag to attach to the document (optional)

file.description: The file to upload (any format supported)

order_number.description: Order number to find the associated dossier. We always use last order by created_at to get dossier.

application_id.description: used to find dossier via order_number and application_id.

name.description: Custom name for the document (optional, defaults to original filename)
